packageEntries.signature: renderToSVG(program, options?: { title?: string; description?: string }): string
renderToPDF(program, options: { output: string; metadata?: PDFMetadata }): Promise<PDFRenderResult>
packageEntries.result: { output: absolutePath; width: logicalWidth; height: logicalHeight; pages: 1; bytes: byteLength }

optionTypes.name: PDFMetadata
optionTypes.shape: { title?: string; author?: string; subject?: string; keywords?: readonly string[] }
optionTypes.policy: Metadata is optional and does not affect graphicSpec or page layout.

decisions.reason: SVG remains usable without DOM, filesystem, or the Node-only native dependency.
It matches the existing PNG file adapter while preserving vector output.
Vector outputs have no raster density and remain comparable to Canvas logical coordinates.
Searchability and small files are more useful than exact glyph-outline portability in the initial scope.
A renderer entry should not silently support only a subset of charts already materialized by ggaction.
PDF metadata and backend encoding can vary while observable content remains equivalent.

nonGoals: SVG DOM mounting or filesystem writing
custom font registration or embedding API
renderer-side semantic inference or text layout
new graphic primitives or public actions
